items.title: UK joins IEA members in coordinated oil stock release
items.summary: UK joins International Energy Agency members in coordinated oil stock release.

items.title: Guidance: Capture Redress Scheme: legal costs framework
items.summary: This guidance sets out the legal costs the Department for Business and Trade (DBT) will cover for Capture Redress Scheme applicants.

items.title: Policy paper: Advanced nuclear framework
items.summary: This Framework is designed to support the development, commercialisation and deployment of private innovative nuclear projects within the UK energy system.

items.title: Accelerating electricity network connections for strategic demand
items.summary: We're seeking views on proposals to amend the connections process to address speculation and prioritise future capacity for strategic demand, including data centres.

items.title: Research: Green home finance products: homeowner awareness and attitudes
items.summary: Quantitative research exploring British homeowners’ awareness and willingness to use green finance products for home decarbonisation.

items.title: Research: Support for green finance and messengers among UK homeowners
items.summary: Quantitative research to explore homeowners’ willingness to use green home finance and trusted messengers on home decarbonisation.

items.title: Research: Comfort taking: evidence review
items.summary: A rapid evidence assessment exploring ‘comfort taking’, where households choose to heat their home to a higher temperature following installation of a home decarbonisation measure.

items.title: Research: Home energy advice services in England: hard-to-reach and digitally excluded consumers
items.summary: Social research to understand the scope of energy advice provision in England, and the preferences of digitally excluded and hard-to-reach consumers.

items.title: Research: Home energy information, advice and guidance
items.summary: Qualitative research exploring perceptions of home energy advice provision and the customer journey in seeking energy advice.

items.title: Research: Home decarbonisation: the role of the residential sales and lettings sector
items.summary: Behavioural research exploring the current and potential role of the sales and lettings sector in supporting homeowners to decarbonise their homes.

items.title: Research: Green home finance consumer typologies and barriers
items.summary: Behavioural research exploring and categorising owner-occupiers’ and private landlords' attitudes to green home finance products.

items.title: Research: Consumer Advice and Information programme: evaluation
items.summary: The final evaluation of the consumer advice and information programme, exploring successes and challenges, outcomes and value-for-money.

items.title: Research: Decarbonising owner-occupied homes for vulnerable groups
items.summary: Qualitative research exploring the barriers to installation of home decarbonisation measures for vulnerable groups, and how these might be overcome.

items.title: Research: Understanding mixed tenure in English social housing
items.summary: Behavioural research to understand the challenges in decarbonising mixed tenure social housing in England.

items.title: Research: Monitoring report: UK-Australia, UK-New Zealand and UK-Japan FTAs
items.summary: This report covers the first 2 years of the UK’s free trade agreements (FTAs) with Australia and New Zealand and the first 4 years of the UK’s FTA with Japan.

items.title: Guidance: Open general export licence military goods: collaborative project Typhoon
items.summary: Licence for the export of military goods for the production, development or maintenance of Typhoon aircraft.

items.title: Notice: Notice to exporters 2026/05: open general export licence (OGEL) updated
items.summary: The Export Control Joint Unit (ECJU) has updated the open general export licence (OGEL) military goods: collaborative project Typhoon.

items.title: Transparency data: Horizon redress schemes: progress reports for 2025
items.summary: Reports on how redress in the Group Litigation Order (GLO) Scheme, the Horizon Convictions Redress Scheme (HCRS) and the Horizon Shortfall Scheme Appeals (HSSA) process are progressing.

items.title: Government to tackle speculative demand grid connection requests
items.summary: Reforms will strengthen conditions for joining and remaining in the queue for demand connections, and prioritise projects that deliver growth and jobs.

items.title: Guidance: Taking part in the UK Emissions Trading Scheme markets
items.summary: This guidance explains the operation of the UK ETS markets and how to take part in auctions and in trading on the secondary market.

items.title: UK manufacturers to save millions of pounds a year as government removes offshore wind energy tariffs
items.summary: British manufacturers will save millions of pounds a year as the Government removes tariffs on offshore wind energy components from 1 April.

items.title: Statutory guidance: Reference Documents for The Customs Tariff (Preferential Trade Arrangements) (EU Exit) Regulations 2020
items.summary: Find the UK's preferential tariffs and Rules of Origin for the agreements contained within the Customs Tariff (Preferential Trade Arrangements) (EU Exit) Regulations 2020.

items.title: Start a new job: step by step
items.summary: What to do when starting a job: checks your employer may do, employment contracts, what to do with your P45 or starter checklist.

items.title: Guidance: Contracts for Difference, renewables obligation and small scale feed-in tariffs: companies awarded exemption or compensation
items.summary: Companies awarded relief for the indirect cost of Contracts for Difference (CfD), renewables obligation (RO) and small scale feed-in tariffs (FIT).

items.title: Guidance: Contracts for Difference, renewables obligation and small scale feed-in tariffs: companies awarded exemption or compensation
items.summary: Companies awarded relief for the indirect cost of Contracts for Difference (CfD), renewables obligation (RO) and small scale feed-in tariffs (FIT).

items.title: Transparency data: Post Office Horizon financial redress and legal costs data for 2025
items.summary: Data for 2025 on redress for postmasters impacted by the Post Office Horizon scandal.

items.title: Research: Capacity Market auction: Auction Monitor report for T-1 auction for 2026 to 2027
items.summary: Capacity Market Auction Monitor report for 2026 to 2027 delivery year.

items.title: Accredited official statistics: Weekly road fuel prices
items.summary: Road fuel price statistics providing average UK retail 'pump' prices on a weekly basis.

items.title: Tough new rules force suppliers to fix faulty smart meters
items.summary: Families to save on their energy bills with new obligations for suppliers to fix smart meters more quickly, cutting waiting times for billpayers.

items.title: Smart metering policy framework post 2025
items.summary: We're seeking views on obligations for energy suppliers to improve smart meter operations and  complete the domestic smart meter rollout by the end of 2030.

items.title: Transparency data: Post Office Capture financial redress data for 2026
items.summary: Data for 2026 on redress for postmasters impacted by the Post Office Capture software.

items.title: Transparency data: Post Office Horizon financial redress and legal costs data for 2026
items.summary: Data for 2026 on redress for postmasters impacted by the Post Office Horizon scandal.

items.title: Make Work Pay: improving access to flexible working
items.summary: We are seeking views on a new process for employers to follow when handling flexible working requests, as well as insights on flexible working practices more broadly.

items.title: Policy paper: UK support to Ukraine: factsheet
items.summary: This factsheet summarises how the UK is supporting Ukraine following the Russian invasion.

items.title: Transparency data: UK-Norway, Iceland, and Liechtenstein FTA Sub-Committee on Sanitary and Phytosanitary measures: joint minutes, 17 November 2025
items.summary: Summary of the third meeting of the UK-Norway, Iceland, and Liechtenstein FTA Sub-Committee on Sanitary and Phytosanitary measures.

items.title: Impact assessment: UK-India Free Trade Agreement: impact assessment
items.summary: This impact assessment sets out the potential economic, sectoral, distributional and environmental impacts of the UK-India Free Trade Agreement.

items.title: Businesses urged to take simple steps for smoother trade with the EU
items.summary: Businesses are being urged to take simple steps to prepare for the landmark EU agri-food deal, that will cut costs, slash red tape and open up opportunities for exporters and importers.

items.title: Statutory guidance: The Provision of Services (Amendment and Transitional Provisions) Regulations 2026
items.summary: This statutory instrument (SI) amends requirements placed on regulators and local authorities by the Provision of Services Regulations 2009.

items.title: Decision: UK-Japan CEPA documents
items.summary: Decisions, documents and meeting minutes from the UK-Japan Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA).

items.title: Provision of Services Regulations 2009: proposed reforms
items.summary: We are seeking views on proposed reforms to the Provision of Services Regulations 2009, under the Retained EU Law programme.
